meta.title: STAMPE2-Score
meta.cff_info:
STAMPE2 Score

Wirsching et al., 2016

DOI: 10.1093/neuonc/nov303

Factors and Points

  • Sensorimotor Deficit: 1 point
  • Tumor Progression: 1 point
  • Age:
    • <55 years: 1 point
  • Major Surgical Complication: 2 points
  • Preoperative Epilepsy: 2 points
  • Epileptiform Potentials on Postoperative EEG: 2 points
  • Edema: 1 point

Risk Estimates

No specific risk estimates were provided in the source data. However, the authors recommend initiating anti-seizure medication (ASM) at a score of 2 points or higher.

Additional Study Information

Category Details
Population Derivation: 779 patients, Zurich (2000-2013).
Statistical Methods Binary logistic regression model.
Performance Metrics Odds Ratios (OR) for key variables were provided:
  • Preoperative Epilepsy OR: 3.46 (95% CI: 2.32-5.16)
  • CNS Infection OR: 5.89 (95% CI: 1.53-22.61)
Generalizability Moderate (limited by retrospective design).
Clinical Utility Guides postoperative anti-seizure medication (ASM) treatment but does not provide specific risk percentages or follow-up periods.
meta.authors: Wirsching H, Morel C, Gmür C, et al.
meta.outcome: Predicting outcome of epilepsy after meningioma resection

predictors.name: Sensorimotor Deficit
predictors.assess: Presence of motor or sensory impairments caused by neurological damage.

predictors.name: Tumor Progression
predictors.assess: Increase in tumor size or activity after initial treatment.

predictors.name: Age < 55 Years
predictors.assess: Younger age at presentation had a higher risk for postoperative epilepsy (OR 1.74)

predictors.name: Major Surgical Complication
predictors.assess: Includes significant postoperative complications such as hydrocephalus, CNS infection, or recraniotomy.

predictors.name: Preoperative Epilepsy
predictors.assess: Documented epileptic seizures occurring before surgery.

predictors.name: Epileptiform Potentials on Postoperative EEG
predictors.assess: Abnormal EEG findings indicating potential epileptic activity after surgery.

predictors.name: Edema
predictors.assess: Edema refers to the swelling around the tumor site, commonly detected as hypodensity on CT scans or T2 hyperintensity on MRI.

recommendation.text: Low risk — no immediate action needed

provenance.notes: STAMPE2: score sum -> author recommendation at threshold >=2 (Initiate ASM). From CFF export; verify vs live form.
